notes.title: Field Notebook
notes.description: Write down your reminders, tasks, and important observations about your crop. Notes are saved per property.
notes.placeholder: e.g., Check irrigation of plot 3 tomorrow morning...
notes.saveSuccessDescription: Your notes have been saved successfully.

locationManager.formAlert: Please fill in all fields with valid values.
locationManager.editCropDescription: Select the crop planted at this location.

mainView.errorTitle: An error occurred while fetching data.
mainView.errorDescription: Could not load data for {locationName}. Please check your connection or coordinates and try again.
mainView.soilDescription: Hourly forecast for temperature and moisture at different depths.
mainView.clickForDetails: Click on cards to see detailed data

details.tempDescription: Temperature affects growth rates and flowering. Very high temperatures can cause heat stress, while very low ones can slow development or cause frost damage.
details.precipDescription: Water is vital for nutrient transport. Excessive rain can leach the soil and cause diseases, while lack of rain requires close irrigation monitoring to prevent wilting.
details.windDescription: Moderate winds help with ventilation and reduction of leaf diseases. Strong winds can topple plants, break branches, and increase water loss through transpiration, besides making spraying difficult.
details.humidityDescription: High humidity favors the appearance of fungi and bacteria. Very low humidity increases the plant's thirst and can impair pollination in some crops.
details.soilTempDescription: Soil temperature affects seed germination and root activity. Roots in very cold soils have difficulty absorbing nutrients.
details.soilMoistureDescription: Soil moisture is the plant's water reservoir. Monitoring deeper layers helps understand if the plant has reserves to withstand periods without rain.
details.radiationDescription: MJ/m² stands for Mega Joules per square meter. It measures the total solar energy reaching your crop during the day. Higher values mean more energy for plant growth and photosynthesis. The ideal solar irradiation requirement will vary depending on the type and variety of the plant.
details.waterBalanceDescription: Water Balance is the difference between water entering (rain) and water leaving (evapotranspiration). Positive values indicate water gain, while negative values indicate loss. Important: a negative balance does not mean the plant will die immediately; plants have adaptation mechanisms (such as closing stomata to avoid excessive water loss) and use water reserves stored in the soil pores. However, a persistent negative balance signals water stress, which reduces growth and final productivity.

alerts.frostMessage: Frost risk with a temperature of {temp}°C forecast for {date}.
